channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Power Pair: The rabbi and the politician
item.description: Rabbi Marcia Zimmerman, the senior rabbi of Minnesota’s largest synagogue, and Frank Hornstein, a former representative in the Minnesota House, have been married for 42 years. Coming up at 9 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court hears Texas death row inmate’s appeal for DNA test of evidence
item.description: Ruben Gutierrez wants to be able to sue prosecutors to compel them to test evidence that he says will prove he did not kill a Brownsville woman in 1998.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Musk endorse Vivek Ramaswamy for Ohio governor
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk on Monday separately endorsed entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy in Ohio's 2026 gubernatorial race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smog checks every 2 years good enough under new proposal at Nevada Legislature
item.description: Smog checks would be required once every two years instead of annually under legislation sponsored by a Democrat and a Republican in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK’s car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Hill was born in 1932 and grew up in Washburn, N.D. He attended Concordia College in Moorhead, Minn., served in the Army and worked as a railroad agent before joining the Secret Service in 1958.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As time change nears, Nevada considers opting out of daylight saving
item.description: Don't have a heart attack, but daylight saving time is less than two weeks away. If a Nevada lawmaker has her way, it will be the last time the state "springs ahead.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ron DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor and takes a shot at Trump's pick
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis approaches the end of his second term in office, he’s talking up one potential heir to succeed him in 2026: his wife, Casey DeSantis. And he's taking a shot at President Donald Trump 's pick to be the next governor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK's car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Clint Hill received Secret Service awards and was promoted for his actions that day, but for decades blamed himself for Kennedy's death.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky Senate hopeful Daniel Cameron praises Trump, criticizes his mentor, Mitch McConnell
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Republican Senate candidate Daniel Cameron lavished praise Monday on Donald Trump and criticized his mentor Sen.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: A federal judge has refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press’ access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not shown it had suffered irreparable harm in the matter.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first interview with the tech genius using AI to discover alleged government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Lottery remains in lawmakers’ sights despite move to ban third-party sales of tickets
item.description: The policy shift was announced before state senators, skeptical of the move, grilled officials with the department and courier services in a hearing on SB 28.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 years on, here’s what to keep an eye on in the Ukraine war
item.description: Thousands of civilians have been killed since Russia launched its full-scale invasion on Feb. 24, 2022. Amid a stark shift in U.S. policy, Ukrainians want the war to end — but not on Russia’s terms.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, Joanna Kakissis, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Greenpeace faces a $300 million lawsuit after Dakota Access Pipeline protests
item.description: The company behind the controversial Dakota Access Pipeline is suing Greenpeace for at least $300 million for damages the oil pipeline company says it suffered from protests in 2016 and 2017.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: DOGE claims it has saved billions, but nearly 75% unaccounted for
item.description: DOGE hasn't provided evidence for approximately $38 billion in alleged savings — nearly 75% of its total claim.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: 2,000 USAID staff to be cut, others put on administrative leave
item.description: “There is no one left to do any work,” the USAID official said, requesting anonymity to protect against retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk blurs the line between his government and business roles
item.description: The tech titan and President Trump say they will avoid any conflicts of interest, but it’s difficult for the public to verify that.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Here's what to know about Germany's election this Sunday
item.description: An economic slump, an immigration crisis and the lifting of a security blanket provided for decades by the U.S. are issues on the minds of German voters. The far-right AfD is polling in second place.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
